title: Create mirroring bash script with framework support
instructions: Create a Bash script at SYSTEM/mirdir.sh that mirrors two directories specified by -s <source> and -d <destination>. The script should follow the framework pattern using PCA for argument parsing, including help, validation, and rsync mirroring.

1779841195.834452.instruction: Create the script file with shebang, framework sourcing, and PCA argument configuration for source and destination.

log.text: Created SYSTEM/mirdir.sh with shebang line, framework sourcing, and PCA argument configuration for source and destination.

1779841197.126782.instruction: Add logic to validate source directory exists, create destination if needed, and perform mirroring with rsync.

log.text: Implemented directory validation logic to check source directory existence and create destination directory if needed. Added rsync command for mirroring with archive, delete, and verbose options.

1779841198.3133862.instruction: Implement proper error handling for all validation steps and ensure correct exit codes are returned.

log.text: Added comprehensive error handling for all validation steps including source directory check, destination directory creation, and proper exit codes propagation.

1779841199.3643775.instruction: Add descriptive comments throughout the script explaining each section and functionality.

log.text: Added comprehensive comments throughout the script to explain the purpose of each section, variable, and command for better maintainability.
